objective: Finish Sage as a complete, hard-cut, bridge-native runtime with a bounded SDK, fresh bridge-native execution services, runtime simplification, and end-to-end cleanup.

milestones.title: Capture intake snapshot and touched scope
notes: Work item scaffolded from the local workflow generator.

milestones.title: Implement the scoped changes
notes: Replaced the duplicated bridge contract/injection path with a manifest-driven SDK, expanded the bounded Discord, moderation, and schedule surfaces, and removed stale multi-tool approval-era runtime branches.

milestones.title: Run validation and publish the completion report
notes: Validated artifacts, docs, lint, typecheck, full tests, build, tools audit, provider-free smoke, trust, and mutation coverage.

progress.summary: Created the local work item scaffold.
Completed the bridge-native completion pass and cleared the remaining audit-only legacy test seams.

decision_log.decision: Keep the public runtime hard-cut to one model-facing capability while expanding the bounded SDK behind Code Mode.
decision_log.rationale: Reintroducing generic tool catalogs or public multi-tool batching would undo the bridge-native product model and re-open the complexity this rewrite is explicitly removing.

discoveries.summary: The remaining failing trust gate was caused by obsolete skipped tests for the retired multi-tool and pre-approval branches, not by active runtime regressions.
evidence: npm run test passed after the runtime changes.
npm run test:audit failed specifically on skipped legacy tests and weak-only manifest assertions until those outdated cases were removed or tightened.

validation_strategy: Run the local workflow artifact validators.
Run lint, typecheck, full Vitest, build, docs checks, tool audit, provider-free bridge smoke, trust, and mutation coverage.
Remove generated non-canonical local output before closeout.
notes: This work intentionally kept the runtime on a bounded SDK surface and did not reintroduce deprecated generic tool-dispatch behavior.
